content: I will start by conducting comprehensive research on San Francisco to ensure the itinerary is grounded in real-time data.
This includes searching for low-budget yet highly-rated accommodation, identifying the best vegetarian-friendly mid-range dining options
, and finding nature, photography, and coffee shop locations that align with the user's "Active Explorer" pace.
I will also investigate rental car options from Santa Clara to San Francisco and check the typical weather for late April to provide accurate
travel tips. Once I have a solid estimate of costs and a map of the best locations, I will commit the
`START` event and proceed with the detailed day-by-day planning.

{"pageTitle":"13 Best Restaurants for Vegetarians in San Francisco","pageLink":"https://sf.eater.com/maps/best-vegetarian-restaurants-san-francisco","pageSnippet":"13 Best Restaurants for Vegetarians in San Francisco · Greens Restaurant · Enjoy Vegetarian · Mamahuhu · Rad Radish · Beit Rima · Shizen · Savor Cafe · Mr. Singh's ...","pageContent":"Here are some of the best restaurants in San Francisco that offer excellent vegetarian and vegan options:

*   **Greens Restaurant**: Since 1979, this landmark vegetarian restaurant has been serving dishes with pristine local produce, including spring rolls, salads, and pizzas. It also offers stunning views of the bay and Marin Headlands.
    *   Address: Building A, San Francisco, CA 94123, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 771-6222
    *   Website: http://www.greensrestaurant.com/

*   **Enjoy Vegetarian**: This Chinese-influenced spot is run by a family of devout Buddhists and is also kosher. It's particularly known for its flavorful soups and offers a second location in the Sunset district.
    *   Address: 839 Kearny St, San Francisco, CA 94108, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 956-7868
    *   Website: http://enjoyveggie.com/

*   **Mamahuhu**: A fast-casual spot from Mister Jiu’s chef Brandon Jew, featuring items like a vegan boba tea-inspired soft-serve sundae and sweet and sour cauliflower. Offers hearty and affordable vegetarian options.
    *   Address: 517 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 94118, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 742-4958
    *   Website: http://www.eatmamahuhu.com/

*   **Rad Radish**: Offers vegetarian breakfast (fully vegan) and lunch options, including chilaquiles, sandwiches, burgers, and flatbreads.
    *   Address: 301 Hayes St, San Francisco, CA 94102, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 916-8548
    *   Website: http://www.eatradradish.com/

*   **Beit Rima**: Known for its exceptional hummus, falafel, and mezze, this restaurant has a largely vegetarian-friendly menu. Must-orders include ful, shakshuka, and delicata squash.
    *   Address: 138 Church St, San Francisco, CA 94114, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 703-0270
    *   Website: http://beitrimasf.com/

*   **Shizen**: A vegan sushi bar and izakaya offering rolls filled with tofu and topped with imitation fish eggs, as well as tempura-fried veggies, bean curd katsu, and miso ramen.
    *   Address: 370 14th St, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 678-5767
    *   Website: http://www.shizensf.com/

*   **Savor Cafe**: Offers a changing menu of vegetarian and vegan dishes, such as wild mushroom knaffe, potato and sage torte, butternut squash kibbe, and jackfruit shawarma.
    *   Address: 401 Irving St, San Francisco, CA 94122, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 702-6048
    *   Website: https://savor.menu/

*   **Mr. Singh’s Curry Pizza**: Offers vegetarian pizzas, including chile paneer pie and a gourmet veggie pizza with ginger and cilantro.
    *   Address: 2241 Judah Street, San Francisco, California 94122
    *   Phone: 1-415-571-8102
    *   Website: https://mrsinghscurrypizza.com/

*   **Cha-Ya Vegetarian Japanese Restaurant**: Serves vegan Japanese fare, including salads, noodle dishes, and inventive sushi rolls.
    *   Address: 762 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 252-7825
    *   Website: https://www.chayasf.com/

*   **Udupi Palace**: A South Indian restaurant with giant dosas, spicy curries, and sweet chutneys. Entree prices are affordable.
    *   Address: 1007 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 970-8000
    *   Website: https://udupisf.com/

*   **3rd Cousin**: This Bernal Heights restaurant offers vegetarian items like potato leek soup, casarecce, and porcini mushroom and black truffle ravioli. A vegan tasting menu is also available.
    *   Address: 919 Cortland Ave, San Francisco, CA 94110, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 814-3709
    *   Website: http://www.3rdcousinsf.com/

*   **Dinosaurs**: Offers two vegetarian banh mi options (crispy tofu or roasted portobello) and tofu and taro spring rolls.
    *   Address: 2522 Ocean Ave, San Francisco, CA 94132, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 334-1421
    *   Website: http://eatdinosaurs.com/

*   **Imperial Garden Seafood Restaurant**: Features vegan dim sum, including vegan-ized siu mai, soy-based shrimp dumplings, and xiao long bao.
    *   Address: 2626 San Bruno Ave, San Francisco, CA 94134, USA
    *   Phone: (415) 468-9333
    *   Website: {"pageTitle":"15 OF THE BEST HIKES IN SAN FRANCISCO AND BEYOND","pageLink":"https://www.chelseyexplores.com/best-hikes-in-san-francisco/","pageSnippet":"The King's Mountain Loop Trail is 3.3 miles long and is considered a moderate hike. The Windy Hill Preserve area offers multiple trails. You can connect ...","pageContent":"*   **Land's End:**
    *   Offers spectacular views of the Golden Gate Bridge and the ocean.
    *   Features a 4-mile leisurely walk with an elevation gain of about 500 ft.
    *   Includes offshoots to lookouts, a labyrinth, and a private beach.
    *   Trailhead accessible from the parking lot at the end of Geary Avenue near Sutro Baths.
    *   Sutro Baths area has historical significance with old photos and a gift shop.
*   **Mount Sutro Forest:**
    *   A hike located within the city, offering a shaded escape with beautiful, old trees (over 100 years old).
    *   Features a 900ft hill, providing a sense of being away from the city.
*   **Twin Peaks:**
    *   Located within San Francisco, offering incredible skyline views.
    *   Can be reached by car or by hiking.
    *   Recommended to visit at sunset for the views.
    *   Be prepared for strong winds and cold weather at the summit.
*   **Alamere Falls:**
    *   A unique hike where a waterfall flows directly into the ocean beach.
    *   Accessible via the Coast Trail from the Palomarin Trailhead, outside Bolinas.
    *   Intermediate hike, well-marked.
    *   Offers views from both the top and bottom of the falls.
    *   Caution: Do not attempt to hike down the ridge to the beach during high tides due to slippery rocks.
*   **Muir Woods:**
    *   Located just over the Golden Gate Bridge, a National Monument known for old-growth coast redwoods.
    *   Can get crowded on weekends; a shuttle service is available due to limited parking.
    *   Expect cold weather due to the shaded environment and proximity to a creek.
    *   No cell service within Muir Woods.
    *   Offers a variety of hikes, from short and easy to long and strenuous.
*   **Point Reyes National Seashore:**
    *   Offers numerous activities and photo opportunities.
    *   Must-visit spots include the lighthouse, an abandoned ship, the Cypress Tree Tunnel, and an Elk Reserve.
*   **Castle Rock:**
    *   Located in Castle Rock State Park, known for sculpted sandstone, forests, and vistas.
    *   Activities include hiking, rock climbing, backpacking, and horseback riding.
    *   There is a $10 parking fee (cash recommended).
*   **King’s Mountain & Windy Hill Preserve:**
    *   Located near Redwood City (approx. 40 minutes south of San Francisco).
    *   Spring is recommended for vibrant green grass.
    *   King’s Mountain Loop Trail is 3.3 miles and moderate.
    *   Windy Hill Preserve has multiple connectable trails for customizable trip lengths.
*   **Sugarloaf Ridge:**
    *   A state park with scenic hikes.
    *   Offers stargazing hikes (guided tours with snacks and telescopes available).
    *   The Vista Trail loop is about 4 miles.
    *   If hiking at night, bring a headlamp for the descent.
*   **McArthur-Burney Falls:**
    *   Located a significant drive from San Francisco, but considered worth it.
    *   The main hike to the falls is about 1.3 miles round trip, with other trails in the area.
    *   Considered an intermediate hike due to a steep downhill start.
    *   Features a 129-foot waterfall.
    *   Theodore Roosevelt called these falls the "eighth wonder of the world."
    *   Recommend winter gear for December visits due to cold temperatures; summer visits may be more pleasant.
*   **Armstrong Woods:**
    *   A less crowded hiking and backpacking area.
    *   The Armstrong Woods to Manning Flats hike is 8 miles roundtrip and doable in a day.
    *   Backpacking permits are required.
*   **Pinnacles National Park:**
    *   Located about 2 hours south of San Francisco, a unique park for a day trip.
    *   Formed by volcanic activity, featuring "pinnacles."
    *   Offers caves, waterfalls, oak woodlands, and canyon bottoms.
    *   Possibility of spotting an Eagle.
    *   Various trail lengths available; camping is also an option.
*   **Big Sur:**
    *   Known for its incredible Highway 1 drive and gorgeous scenery.
    *   Hiking options include Pfeiffer Falls and trails around Bixby Bridge.
    *   Vicente Flats hike is an intermediate, 10-mile out-and-back trail.
    *   Backpacking is possible, but the trail can be done in a day.
*   **Tahoe:**
    *   About 4 hours from San Francisco, with distinct summer and winter landscapes.
    *   Summer offers numerous hiking trails.
    *   Winter allows for snowshoeing.
    *   Personal experience includes Cascade Falls Trail (1.4 miles) and Bayview Trail (4.2 miles, intermediate).
*   **YOSEMITE:**
    *   Located in NorCal, a spectacular park with endless hiking trails.
    *   Features unique rock formations and stunning waterfalls.
    *   Highly recommended if visiting the Bay Area and have time.","pageExternalLinks":["https://www.chelseyexplores.com/san-diego-adventure-quiz/","https://www.nps.gov/muwo/index.htm","https://www.nps.gov/pore/index.htm","https://www.territorysupply.com/point-reyes-hikes","https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=538","https://sugarloafpark.org/activities/stargazing/","https://www.instagram.com/chelseyexplores/","https://www.chelseyexplores.com/backpacking-for-thanksgiving-a-c-s-r-park/","https://www.chelseyexplores.com/weekend-big-sur-backpacking-trip/"],"pageStatus":"Success","hasNext":true,"nextIndex":1}
